types.name: Annotations
types.description: Optional annotations for the client. The client can use annotations to inform how objects are used or displayed

fields.name: audience
fields.description:
Describes who the intended audience of this object or data is.

It can include multiple entries to indicate content useful for multiple audiences (e.g., `["user", "assistant"]`).

fields.name: lastModified
fields.description:
The moment the resource was last modified, as an ISO 8601 formatted string.

Should be an ISO 8601 formatted string (e.g., "2025-01-12T15:00:58Z").

Examples: last activity timestamp in an open file, timestamp when the resource
was attached, etc.

fields.name: priority
fields.description:
Describes how important this data is for operating the server.

A value of 1 means "most important," and indicates that the data is
effectively required, while 0 means "least important," and indicates that
the data is entirely optional.

types.name: AudioContent
types.description: Audio provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The base64-encoded audio data.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of the audio. Different providers may support different audio types.

types.name: BaseMetadata
types.description: Base interface for metadata with name (identifier) and title (display name) properties.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: blob
fields.description: A base64-encoded string representing the binary data of the item.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: CallToolRequest
types.description: Used by the client to invoke a tool provided by the server.

types.name: CallToolRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `tools/call` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: Arguments to use for the tool call.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the tool.

fields.name: task
fields.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

types.name: CallToolResult
types.description: The server's response to a tool call.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: content
fields.description: A list of content objects that represent the unstructured result of the tool call.

fields.name: isError
fields.description:
Whether the tool call ended in an error.

If not set, this is assumed to be false (the call was successful).

Any errors that originate from the tool SHOULD be reported inside the result
object, with `isError` set to true, _not_ as an MCP protocol-level error
response. Otherwise, the LLM would not be able to see that an error occurred
and self-correct.

However, any errors in _finding_ the tool, an error indicating that the
server does not support tool calls, or any other exceptional conditions,
should be reported as an MCP error response.

fields.name: structuredContent
fields.description: An optional JSON object that represents the structured result of the tool call.

types.name: CancelTaskRequest
types.description: A request to cancel a task.

fields.name: taskId
fields.description: The task identifier to cancel.

types.name: CancelTaskResult
types.description: The response to a tasks/cancel request.

types.name: CancelledNotification
types.description:
This notification can be sent by either side to indicate that it is cancelling a previously-issued request.

The request SHOULD still be in-flight, but due to communication latency, it is always possible that this notification MAY arrive after the request has already finished.

This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.

A client MUST NOT attempt to cancel its `initialize` request.

For task cancellation, use the `tasks/cancel` request instead of this notification.

types.name: CancelledNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/cancelled` notification.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: An optional string describing the reason for the cancellation. This MAY be logged or presented to the user.

fields.name: requestId
fields.description:
The ID of the request to cancel.

This MUST correspond to the ID of a request previously issued in the same direction.
This MUST be provided for cancelling non-task requests.
This MUST NOT be used for cancelling tasks (use the `tasks/cancel` request instead).

types.name: ClientCapabilities
types.description: Capabilities a client may support. Known capabilities are defined here, in this schema, but this is not a closed set: any client can define its own, additional capabilities.

fields.name: elicitation
fields.description: Present if the client supports elicitation from the server.

fields.name: experimental
fields.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.

fields.name: roots
listChanged
fields.description: Whether the client supports notifications for changes to the roots list.
Present if the client supports listing roots.

fields.name: sampling
context
fields.description:
Whether the client supports context inclusion via includeContext parameter.
If not declared, servers SHOULD only use `includeContext: "none"` (or omit it).

fields.name: tools
fields.description: Whether the client supports tool use via tools and toolChoice parameters.
Present if the client supports sampling from an LLM.

fields.name: tasks
cancel
fields.description: Whether this client supports tasks/cancel.

fields.name: list
fields.description: Whether this client supports tasks/list.

fields.name: requests
elicitation
create
fields.description: Whether the client supports task-augmented elicitation/create requests.
Task support for elicitation-related requests.

fields.name: sampling
createMessage
fields.description: Whether the client supports task-augmented sampling/createMessage requests.
Task support for sampling-related requests.
Specifies which request types can be augmented with tasks.
Present if the client supports task-augmented requests.

types.name: CompleteRequest
types.description: A request from the client to the server, to ask for completion options.

types.name: CompleteRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `completion/complete` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: argument
name
fields.description: The name of the argument

fields.name: value
fields.description: The value of the argument to use for completion matching.
The argument's information

fields.name: context
arguments
fields.description: Previously-resolved variables in a URI template or prompt.
Additional, optional context for completions

types.name: CompleteResult
types.description: The server's response to a completion/complete request

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: hasMore
fields.description: Indicates whether there are additional completion options beyond those provided in the current response, even if the exact total is unknown.

fields.name: total
fields.description: The total number of completion options available. This can exceed the number of values actually sent in the response.

fields.name: values
fields.description: An array of completion values. Must not exceed 100 items.

types.name: CreateMessageRequest
types.description: A request from the server to sample an LLM via the client. The client has full discretion over which model to select. The client should also inform the user before beginning sampling, to allow them to inspect the request (human in the loop) and decide whether to approve it.

types.name: CreateMessageRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `sampling/createMessage` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: includeContext
fields.description:
A request to include context from one or more MCP servers (including the caller), to be attached to the prompt.
The client MAY ignore this request.

Default is "none". Values "thisServer" and "allServers" are soft-deprecated. Servers SHOULD only use these values if the client
declares ClientCapabilities.sampling.context. These values may be removed in future spec releases.

fields.name: maxTokens
fields.description:
The requested maximum number of tokens to sample (to prevent runaway completions).

The client MAY choose to sample fewer tokens than the requested maximum.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional metadata to pass through to the LLM provider. The format of this metadata is provider-specific.

fields.name: modelPreferences
fields.description: The server's preferences for which model to select. The client MAY ignore these preferences.

fields.name: systemPrompt
fields.description: An optional system prompt the server wants to use for sampling. The client MAY modify or omit this prompt.

fields.name: task
fields.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

fields.name: toolChoice
fields.description:
Controls how the model uses tools.
The client MUST return an error if this field is provided but ClientCapabilities.sampling.tools is not declared.
Default is `{ mode: "auto" }`.

fields.name: tools
fields.description:
Tools that the model may use during generation.
The client MUST return an error if this field is provided but ClientCapabilities.sampling.tools is not declared.

types.name: CreateMessageResult
types.description:
The client's response to a sampling/createMessage request from the server.
The client should inform the user before returning the sampled message, to allow them
to inspect the response (human in the loop) and decide whether to allow the server to see it.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: model
fields.description: The name of the model that generated the message.

fields.name: stopReason
fields.description:
The reason why sampling stopped, if known.

Standard values:
- "endTurn": Natural end of the assistant's turn
- "stopSequence": A stop sequence was encountered
- "maxTokens": Maximum token limit was reached
- "toolUse": The model wants to use one or more tools

This field is an open string to allow for provider-specific stop reasons.

types.name: CreateTaskResult
types.description: A response to a task-augmented request.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: Cursor
types.description: An opaque token used to represent a cursor for pagination.

types.name: ElicitRequest
types.description: A request from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

types.name: ElicitRequestFormParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit non-sensitive information from the user via a form in the client.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The message to present to the user describing what information is being requested.

fields.name: mode
fields.description: The elicitation mode.

fields.name: requestedSchema
fields.description:
A restricted subset of JSON Schema.
Only top-level properties are allowed, without nesting.

fields.name: task
fields.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

types.name: ElicitRequestParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit additional information from the user via the client.

types.name: ElicitRequestURLParams
types.description: The parameters for a request to elicit information from the user via a URL in the client.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: elicitationId
fields.description:
The ID of the elicitation, which must be unique within the context of the server.
The client MUST treat this ID as an opaque value.

fields.name: message
fields.description: The message to present to the user explaining why the interaction is needed.

fields.name: mode
fields.description: The elicitation mode.

fields.name: task
fields.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

fields.name: url
fields.description: The URL that the user should navigate to.

types.name: ElicitResult
types.description: The client's response to an elicitation request.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: action
fields.description:
The user action in response to the elicitation.
- "accept": User submitted the form/confirmed the action
- "decline": User explicitly decline the action
- "cancel": User dismissed without making an explicit choice

fields.name: content
fields.description:
The submitted form data, only present when action is "accept" and mode was "form".
Contains values matching the requested schema.
Omitted for out-of-band mode responses.

types.name: ElicitationCompleteNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it of a completion of a out-of-band elicitation request.

fields.name: elicitationId
fields.description: The ID of the elicitation that completed.

types.name: EmbeddedResource
types.description:
The contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.

It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit
of the LLM and/or the user.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: code
fields.description: The error type that occurred.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Additional information about the error. The value of this member is defined by the sender (e.g. detailed error information, nested errors etc.).

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short description of the error. The message SHOULD be limited to a concise single sentence.

types.name: GetPromptRequest
types.description: Used by the client to get a prompt provided by the server.

types.name: GetPromptRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `prompts/get` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: Arguments to use for templating the prompt.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the prompt or prompt template.

types.name: GetPromptResult
types.description: The server's response to a prompts/get request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description for the prompt.

types.name: GetTaskPayloadRequest
types.description: A request to retrieve the result of a completed task.

fields.name: taskId
fields.description: The task identifier to retrieve results for.

types.name: GetTaskPayloadResult
types.description:
The response to a tasks/result request.
The structure matches the result type of the original request.
For example, a tools/call task would return the CallToolResult structure.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: GetTaskRequest
types.description: A request to retrieve the state of a task.

fields.name: taskId
fields.description: The task identifier to query.

types.name: GetTaskResult
types.description: The response to a tasks/get request.

types.name: Icon
types.description: An optionally-sized icon that can be displayed in a user interface.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description:
Optional MIME type override if the source MIME type is missing or generic.
For example: `"image/png"`, `"image/jpeg"`, or `"image/svg+xml"`.

fields.name: sizes
fields.description:
Optional array of strings that specify sizes at which the icon can be used.
Each string should be in WxH format (e.g., `"48x48"`, `"96x96"`) or `"any"` for scalable formats like SVG.

If not provided, the client should assume that the icon can be used at any size.

fields.name: src
fields.description:
A standard URI pointing to an icon resource. May be an HTTP/HTTPS URL or a
`data:` URI with Base64-encoded image data.

Consumers SHOULD takes steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the
same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain.

Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain
executable JavaScript.

fields.name: theme
fields.description:
Optional specifier for the theme this icon is designed for. `light` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a light background, and `dark` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a dark background.

If not provided, the client should assume the icon can be used with any theme.

types.name: Icons
types.description: Base interface to add `icons` property.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

types.name: ImageContent
types.description: An image provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The base64-encoded image data.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of the image. Different providers may support different image types.

types.name: Implementation
types.description: Describes the MCP implementation.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
An optional human-readable description of what this implementation does.

This can be used by clients or servers to provide context about their purpose
and capabilities. For example, a server might describe the types of resources
or tools it provides, while a client might describe its intended use case.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: websiteUrl
fields.description: An optional URL of the website for this implementation.

types.name: InitializeRequest
types.description: This request is sent from the client to the server when it first connects, asking it to begin initialization.

types.name: InitializeRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for an `initialize` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: protocolVersion
fields.description: The latest version of the Model Context Protocol that the client supports. The client MAY decide to support older versions as well.

types.name: InitializeResult
types.description: After receiving an initialize request from the client, the server sends this response.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: instructions
fields.description:
Instructions describing how to use the server and its features.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available tools, resources, etc. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model. For example, this information MAY be added to the system prompt.

fields.name: protocolVersion
fields.description: The version of the Model Context Protocol that the server wants to use. This may not match the version that the client requested. If the client cannot support this version, it MUST disconnect.

types.name: InitializedNotification
types.description: This notification is sent from the client to the server after initialization has finished.

types.name: JSONRPCErrorResponse
types.description: A response to a request that indicates an error occurred.

types.name: JSONRPCMessage
types.description: Refers to any valid JSON-RPC object that can be decoded off the wire, or encoded to be sent.

types.name: JSONRPCNotification
types.description: A notification which does not expect a response.

types.name: JSONRPCRequest
types.description: A request that expects a response.

types.name: JSONRPCResponse
types.description: A response to a request, containing either the result or error.

types.name: JSONRPCResultResponse
types.description: A successful (non-error) response to a request.

types.name: LegacyTitledEnumSchema
types.description:
Use TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema instead.
This interface will be removed in a future version.

fields.name: enumNames
fields.description:
(Legacy) Display names for enum values.
Non-standard according to JSON schema 2020-12.

types.name: ListPromptsRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of prompts and prompt templates the server has.

types.name: ListPromptsResult
types.description: The server's response to a prompts/list request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: ListResourceTemplatesRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of resource templates the server has.

types.name: ListResourceTemplatesResult
types.description: The server's response to a resources/templates/list request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: ListResourcesRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of resources the server has.

types.name: ListResourcesResult
types.description: The server's response to a resources/list request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: ListRootsRequest
types.description:
Sent from the server to request a list of root URIs from the client. Roots allow
servers to ask for specific directories or files to operate on. A common example
for roots is providing a set of repositories or directories a server should operate
on.

This request is typically used when the server needs to understand the file system
structure or access specific locations that the client has permission to read from.

types.name: ListRootsResult
types.description:
The client's response to a roots/list request from the server.
This result contains an array of Root objects, each representing a root directory
or file that the server can operate on.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: ListTasksRequest
types.description: A request to retrieve a list of tasks.

types.name: ListTasksResult
types.description: The response to a tasks/list request.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: ListToolsRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request a list of tools the server has.

types.name: ListToolsResult
types.description: The server's response to a tools/list request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: LoggingLevel
types.description:
The severity of a log message.

These map to syslog message severities, as specified in RFC-5424:
https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5424#section-6.2.1

types.name: LoggingMessageNotification
types.description: JSONRPCNotification of a log message passed from server to client. If no logging/setLevel request has been sent from the client, the server MAY decide which messages to send automatically.

types.name: LoggingMessageNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/message` notification.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The data to be logged, such as a string message or an object. Any JSON serializable type is allowed here.

fields.name: level
fields.description: The severity of this log message.

fields.name: logger
fields.description: An optional name of the logger issuing this message.

types.name: ModelHint
types.description:
Hints to use for model selection.

Keys not declared here are currently left unspecified by the spec and are up
to the client to interpret.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
A hint for a model name.

The client SHOULD treat this as a substring of a model name; for example:
 - `claude-3-5-sonnet` should match `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`
 - `sonnet` should match `claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022`, `claude-3-sonnet-20240229`, etc.
 - `claude` should match any Claude model

The client MAY also map the string to a different provider's model name or a different model family, as long as it fills a similar niche; for example:
 - `gemini-1.5-flash` could match `claude-3-haiku-20240307`

types.name: ModelPreferences
types.description:
The server's preferences for model selection, requested of the client during sampling.

Because LLMs can vary along multiple dimensions, choosing the "best" model is
rarely straightforward.  Different models excel in different areas—some are
faster but less capable, others are more capable but more expensive, and so
on. This interface allows servers to express their priorities across multiple
dimensions to help clients make an appropriate selection for their use case.

These preferences are always advisory. The client MAY ignore them. It is also
up to the client to decide how to interpret these preferences and how to
balance them against other considerations.

fields.name: costPriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize cost when selecting a model. A value of 0 means cost
is not important, while a value of 1 means cost is the most important
factor.

fields.name: hints
fields.description:
Optional hints to use for model selection.

If multiple hints are specified, the client MUST evaluate them in order
(such that the first match is taken).

The client SHOULD prioritize these hints over the numeric priorities, but
MAY still use the priorities to select from ambiguous matches.

fields.name: intelligencePriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize intelligence and capabilities when selecting a
model. A value of 0 means intelligence is not important, while a value of 1
means intelligence is the most important factor.

fields.name: speedPriority
fields.description:
How much to prioritize sampling speed (latency) when selecting a model. A
value of 0 means speed is not important, while a value of 1 means speed is
the most important factor.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: PaginatedRequestParams
types.description: Common parameters for paginated requests.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: cursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the current pagination position.
If provided, the server should return results starting after this cursor.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: nextCursor
fields.description:
An opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
If present, there may be more results available.

types.name: PingRequest
types.description: A ping, issued by either the server or the client, to check that the other party is still alive. The receiver must promptly respond, or else may be disconnected.

types.name: PrimitiveSchemaDefinition
types.description:
Restricted schema definitions that only allow primitive types
without nested objects or arrays.

types.name: ProgressNotification
types.description: An out-of-band notification used to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.

types.name: ProgressNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/progress` notification.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: message
fields.description: An optional message describing the current progress.

fields.name: progress
fields.description: The progress thus far. This should increase every time progress is made, even if the total is unknown.

fields.name: progressToken
fields.description: The progress token which was given in the initial request, used to associate this notification with the request that is proceeding.

fields.name: total
fields.description: Total number of items to process (or total progress required), if known.

types.name: ProgressToken
types.description: A progress token, used to associate progress notifications with the original request.

types.name: Prompt
types.description: A prompt or prompt template that the server offers.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: arguments
fields.description: A list of arguments to use for templating the prompt.

fields.name: description
fields.description: An optional description of what this prompt provides

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: PromptArgument
types.description: Describes an argument that a prompt can accept.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A human-readable description of the argument.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: required
fields.description: Whether this argument must be provided.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: PromptListChangedNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of prompts it offers has changed. This may be issued by servers without any previous subscription from the client.

types.name: PromptMessage
types.description:
Describes a message returned as part of a prompt.

This is similar to `SamplingMessage`, but also supports the embedding of
resources from the MCP server.

types.name: PromptReference
types.description: Identifies a prompt.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: ReadResourceRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to the server, to read a specific resource URI.

types.name: ReadResourceRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `resources/read` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken

The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

types.name: ReadResourceResult
types.description: The server's response to a resources/read request from the client.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: RelatedTaskMetadata
types.description:
Metadata for associating messages with a task.
Include this in the `_meta` field under the key `io.modelcontextprotocol/related-task`.

fields.name: taskId

progressToken

types.name: Resource
types.description: A known resource that the server is capable of reading.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what this resource represents.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: size
fields.description:
The size of the raw resource content, in bytes (i.e., before base64 encoding or any tokenization), if known.

This can be used by Hosts to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

fields.name: mimeType

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: ResourceLink
types.description:
A resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.

Note: resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of `resources/list` requests.

fields.name: _meta

fields.name: description

A description of what this resource represents.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons

Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType

fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: size

The size of the raw resource content, in bytes (i.e., before base64 encoding or any tokenization), if known.

This can be used by Hosts to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.

Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,

fields.name: _meta
progressToken

The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.

types.name: ResourceTemplate

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A description of what this template is for.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type for all resources that match this template. This should only be included if all resources matching this template have the same type.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

fields.name: uriTemplate
fields.description: A URI template (according to RFC 6570) that can be used to construct resource URIs.

types.name: ResourceTemplateReference
types.description: A reference to a resource or resource template definition.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI or URI template of the resource.

types.name: ResourceUpdatedNotification
types.description: A notification from the server to the client, informing it that a resource has changed and may need to be read again. This should only be sent if the client previously sent a resources/subscribe request.

types.name: ResourceUpdatedNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/resources/updated` notification.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource that has been updated. This might be a sub-resource of the one that the client actually subscribed to.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: Role
types.description: The sender or recipient of messages and data in a conversation.

types.name: Root
types.description: Represents a root directory or file that the server can operate on.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: name
fields.description:
An optional name for the root. This can be used to provide a human-readable
identifier for the root, which may be useful for display purposes or for
referencing the root in other parts of the application.

fields.name: uri
fields.description:
The URI identifying the root. This *must* start with file:// for now.
This restriction may be relaxed in future versions of the protocol to allow
other URI schemes.

types.name: RootsListChangedNotification
types.description:
A notification from the client to the server, informing it that the list of roots has changed.
This notification should be sent whenever the client adds, removes, or modifies any root.
The server should then request an updated list of roots using the ListRootsRequest.

types.name: SamplingMessage
types.description: Describes a message issued to or received from an LLM API.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

types.name: ServerCapabilities
types.description: Capabilities that a server may support. Known capabilities are defined here, in this schema, but this is not a closed set: any server can define its own, additional capabilities.

fields.name: completions
fields.description: Present if the server supports argument autocompletion suggestions.

fields.name: experimental
fields.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.

fields.name: logging
fields.description: Present if the server supports sending log messages to the client.

fields.name: prompts
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the prompt list.
Present if the server offers any prompt templates.

fields.name: resources
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the resource list.

fields.name: subscribe
fields.description: Whether this server supports subscribing to resource updates.
Present if the server offers any resources to read.

fields.name: tasks
cancel
fields.description: Whether this server supports tasks/cancel.

fields.name: list
fields.description: Whether this server supports tasks/list.

fields.name: requests
tools
call
fields.description: Whether the server supports task-augmented tools/call requests.
Task support for tool-related requests.
Specifies which request types can be augmented with tasks.
Present if the server supports task-augmented requests.

fields.name: tools
listChanged
fields.description: Whether this server supports notifications for changes to the tool list.
Present if the server offers any tools to call.

types.name: SetLevelRequest
types.description: A request from the client to the server, to enable or adjust logging.

types.name: SetLevelRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `logging/setLevel` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: level
fields.description: The level of logging that the client wants to receive from the server. The server should send all logs at this level and higher (i.e., more severe) to the client as notifications/message.

types.name: SubscribeRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request resources/updated notifications from the server whenever a particular resource changes.

types.name: SubscribeRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `resources/subscribe` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

types.name: Task
types.description: Data associated with a task.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.

fields.name: lastUpdatedAt
fields.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was last updated.

fields.name: pollInterval
fields.description: Suggested polling interval in milliseconds.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Current task state.

fields.name: statusMessage
fields.description:
Optional human-readable message describing the current task state.
This can provide context for any status, including:
- Reasons for "cancelled" status
- Summaries for "completed" status
- Diagnostic information for "failed" status (e.g., error details, what went wrong)

fields.name: taskId
fields.description: The task identifier.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Actual retention duration from creation in milliseconds, null for unlimited.

types.name: TaskAugmentedRequestParams
types.description: Common params for any task-augmented request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: task
fields.description:
If specified, the caller is requesting task-augmented execution for this request.
The request will return a CreateTaskResult immediately, and the actual result can be
retrieved later via tasks/result.

Task augmentation is subject to capability negotiation - receivers MUST declare support
for task augmentation of specific request types in their capabilities.

types.name: TaskMetadata
types.description:
Metadata for augmenting a request with task execution.
Include this in the `task` field of the request parameters.

fields.name: ttl
fields.description: Requested duration in milliseconds to retain task from creation.

types.name: TaskStatus
types.description: The status of a task.

types.name: TaskStatusNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the receiver to the requestor, informing them that a task's status has changed. Receivers are not required to send these notifications.

types.name: TaskStatusNotificationParams
types.description: Parameters for a `notifications/tasks/status` notification.

types.name: TextContent
types.description: Text provided to or from an LLM.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description: Optional annotations for the client.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text content of the message.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: mimeType
fields.description: The MIME type of this resource, if known.

fields.name: text
fields.description: The text of the item. This must only be set if the item can actually be represented as text (not binary data).

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of this resource.

types.name: TitledMultiSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for multiple-selection enumeration with display titles for each option.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: items
anyOf
const
fields.description: The constant enum value.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Display title for this option.
Array of enum options with values and display labels.
Schema for array items with enum options and display labels.

fields.name: maxItems
fields.description: Maximum number of items to select.

fields.name: minItems
fields.description: Minimum number of items to select.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

types.name: TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for single-selection enumeration with display titles for each option.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: oneOf
const
fields.description: The enum value.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Display label for this option.
Array of enum options with values and display labels.

types.name: Tool
types.description: Definition for a tool the client can call.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description: See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: annotations
fields.description:
Optional additional tool information.

Display name precedence order is: title, annotations.title, then name.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
A human-readable description of the tool.

This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available tools. It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model.

fields.name: execution
fields.description: Execution-related properties for this tool.

fields.name: icons
fields.description:
Optional set of sized icons that the client can display in a user interface.

Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)

Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

fields.name: inputSchema
fields.description: A JSON Schema object defining the expected parameters for the tool.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Intended for programmatic or logical use, but used as a display name in past specs or fallback (if title isn't present).

fields.name: outputSchema
fields.description:
An optional JSON Schema object defining the structure of the tool's output returned in
the structuredContent field of a CallToolResult.

Defaults to JSON Schema 2020-12 when no explicit $schema is provided.
Currently restricted to type: "object" at the root level.

fields.name: title
fields.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.

If not provided, the name should be used for display (except for Tool,
where `annotations.title` should be given precedence over using `name`,
if present).

types.name: ToolAnnotations
types.description:
Additional properties describing a Tool to clients.

NOTE: all properties in ToolAnnotations are **hints**.
They are not guaranteed to provide a faithful description of
tool behavior (including descriptive properties like `title`).

Clients should never make tool use decisions based on ToolAnnotations
received from untrusted servers.

fields.name: destructiveHint
fields.description:
If true, the tool may perform destructive updates to its environment.
If false, the tool performs only additive updates.

(This property is meaningful only when `readOnlyHint == false`)

Default: true

fields.name: idempotentHint
fields.description:
If true, calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
will have no additional effect on its environment.

(This property is meaningful only when `readOnlyHint == false`)

Default: false

fields.name: openWorldHint
fields.description:
If true, this tool may interact with an "open world" of external
entities. If false, the tool's domain of interaction is closed.
For example, the world of a web search tool is open, whereas that
of a memory tool is not.

Default: true

fields.name: readOnlyHint
fields.description:
If true, the tool does not modify its environment.

Default: false

fields.name: title
fields.description: A human-readable title for the tool.

types.name: ToolChoice
types.description: Controls tool selection behavior for sampling requests.

fields.name: mode
fields.description:
Controls the tool use ability of the model:
- "auto": Model decides whether to use tools (default)
- "required": Model MUST use at least one tool before completing
- "none": Model MUST NOT use any tools

types.name: ToolExecution
types.description: Execution-related properties for a tool.

fields.name: taskSupport
fields.description:
Indicates whether this tool supports task-augmented execution.
This allows clients to handle long-running operations through polling
the task system.

- "forbidden": Tool does not support task-augmented execution (default when absent)
- "optional": Tool may support task-augmented execution
- "required": Tool requires task-augmented execution

Default: "forbidden"

types.name: ToolListChangedNotification
types.description: An optional notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of tools it offers has changed. This may be issued by servers without any previous subscription from the client.

types.name: ToolResultContent
types.description: The result of a tool use, provided by the user back to the assistant.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description:
Optional metadata about the tool result. Clients SHOULD preserve this field when
including tool results in subsequent sampling requests to enable caching optimizations.

See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: content
fields.description:
The unstructured result content of the tool use.

This has the same format as CallToolResult.content and can include text, images,
audio, resource links, and embedded resources.

fields.name: isError
fields.description:
Whether the tool use resulted in an error.

If true, the content typically describes the error that occurred.
Default: false

fields.name: structuredContent
fields.description:
An optional structured result object.

If the tool defined an outputSchema, this SHOULD conform to that schema.

fields.name: toolUseId
fields.description:
The ID of the tool use this result corresponds to.

This MUST match the ID from a previous ToolUseContent.

types.name: ToolUseContent
types.description: A request from the assistant to call a tool.

fields.name: _meta
fields.description:
Optional metadata about the tool use. Clients SHOULD preserve this field when
including tool uses in subsequent sampling requests to enable caching optimizations.

See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: id
fields.description:
A unique identifier for this tool use.

This ID is used to match tool results to their corresponding tool uses.

fields.name: input
fields.description: The arguments to pass to the tool, conforming to the tool's input schema.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of the tool to call.

types.name: URLElicitationRequiredError
types.description: An error response that indicates that the server requires the client to provide additional information via an elicitation request.

types.name: UnsubscribeRequest
types.description: Sent from the client to request cancellation of resources/updated notifications from the server. This should follow a previous resources/subscribe request.

types.name: UnsubscribeRequestParams
types.description: Parameters for a `resources/unsubscribe` request.

fields.name: _meta
progressToken
fields.description: If specified, the caller is requesting out-of-band progress notifications for this request (as represented by notifications/progress). The value of this parameter is an opaque token that will be attached to any subsequent notifications. The receiver is not obligated to provide these notifications.
See [General fields: `_meta`](/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index#meta) for notes on `_meta` usage.

fields.name: uri
fields.description: The URI of the resource. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.

types.name: UntitledMultiSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for multiple-selection enumeration without display titles for options.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: items
enum
fields.description: Array of enum values to choose from.
Schema for the array items.

fields.name: maxItems
fields.description: Maximum number of items to select.

fields.name: minItems
fields.description: Minimum number of items to select.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

types.name: UntitledSingleSelectEnumSchema
types.description: Schema for single-selection enumeration without display titles for options.

fields.name: default
fields.description: Optional default value.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Optional description for the enum field.

fields.name: enum
fields.description: Array of enum values to choose from.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Optional title for the enum field.

capabilities.name: elicitation
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports elicitation from the server.

capabilities.name: experimental
capabilities.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.

capabilities.name: roots
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports listing roots.

capabilities.name: sampling
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports sampling from an LLM.

capabilities.name: tasks
capabilities.description: Present if the client supports task-augmented requests.

capabilities.name: completions
capabilities.description: Present if the server supports argument autocompletion suggestions.

capabilities.name: experimental
capabilities.description: Experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.

capabilities.name: logging
capabilities.description: Present if the server supports sending log messages to the client.

capabilities.name: prompts
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any prompt templates.

capabilities.name: resources
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any resources to read.

capabilities.name: tasks
capabilities.description: Present if the server supports task-augmented requests.

capabilities.name: tools
capabilities.description: Present if the server offers any tools to call.
